channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Families scramble to help after deadly cyclone rips through French territory of Mayotte off Africa
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — Relatives of families struggling after Cyclone Chido ripped through the French island territory of Mayotte expressed helplessness Wednesday, a day before France’s president and another 180 tons of aid were expected to arrive.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Some Walmarts testing body cameras for employees
item.description: "We are always looking at new and innovative technology used across the retail industry,” a Walmart spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: 184-foot floating Christmas tree collapses, killing 21-year-old man
item.description: A 21-year-old man was killed when a 184-foot floating Christmas tree collapsed in Brazil earlier this week. The incident occurred on Monday in the city of Maricá, which is located roughly 25 miles east of Rio de Janeiro.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Wisconsin police go quiet on school shooting as search for answers continues
item.description: Wisconsin police did not plan any public updates Wednesday into the extremely rare religious school shooting that killed a teacher and a student and wounded six others, a day after the city’s mayor chastised reporters and urged them to leave victims alone.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Scott Bauer And Ryan J. Foley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DeSantis slams 'false media narratives' on illegal immigration response as report shows Florida's economy grew
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis blasted “false media narrative" on illegal immigration after a report showed that the state saw economic growth despite outcry from some Democrats and media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Couple allowed child to go severely malnourished: Police
item.description: Law enforcement arrested an Indiana couple they say allowed a 2-month-old to go severely malnourished.
item.dc:creator: Austin Hanson, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court takes up challenge to TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court announced it will take up whether a law that could ban TikTok nationwide violates the First Amendment, setting the stage for a fast-paced, high-stakes battle over free speech.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Gaetz defends ‘embarrassing’ past behavior, fires shot at Ethics report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) is defending what he says is "embarrassing, though not criminal" past behavior and taking a shot at the House Ethics Committee, following reports that the panel voted to release its findings about him.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man convicted of quadruple homicide is put to death in Indiana's 1st execution in 15 years
item.description: MICHIGAN CITY, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man convicted of killing four people including his brother and his sister’s fiancé decades ago was put to death early Wednesday, marking the state’s first execution in 15 years. Joseph Corcoran, 49, was pronounced dead at 12:44 a.m.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rear seat belt reminders to be required in 2027
item.description: NHTSA estimated the new rule will prevent more than 500 injuries and save about 50 lives each year if and when it's fully implemented.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Family of slain Cash App founder Bob Lee reacts to Nima Momeni verdict
item.description: On Tuesday morning, a San Francisco jury announced it had found Nima Momeni guilty of second degree murder in the death of Cash App founder Bob Lee.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, WGN 9

item.title: FTC bans junk ticket, hotel fees
item.description: The final rule will become effective 120 days after its publication in the Federal Register, coinciding with the first few months of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect charged with killing UnitedHealthcare CEO as an act of terrorism, authorities announce
item.description: Mangione had previously been charged with murder in Thompson's killing, but the terror allegation is new.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to claim a 2nd payment from the Equifax data breach settlement
item.description: Equifax has started alerting people of more money waiting for them from a massive fund the credit reporting agency paid to settle a data breach.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Shock poll: 41 percent of young voters find killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO acceptable
item.description: Anger over health insurance companies has been in the spotlight after Thompson was fatally shot Dec. 4 in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Strange laws in New Mexico, including trouble for tripping a horse
item.description: You may be unaware you're breaking the law with the strange, widely unknown laws in U.S. states. A few of New Mexico's strangest include horses and duels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Alabama woman is doing well after the latest experimental pig kidney transplant
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An Alabama woman is recovering well after a pig kidney transplant last month that freed her from eight years of dialysis, the latest effort to save human lives with animal organs.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grammy award-winning gospel singer arrested in Greenville
item.description: GREENVILLE, S.C. (WSPA) - Grammy award-winning gospel singer Le'Andria Johnson was arrested Monday afternoon in Greenville for public intoxication. The City of Greenville confirmed with 7NEWS that Johnson was charged with public intoxication and an open container on a public street.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect facing murder charges in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings was charged on Tuesday in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, WGN 9

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect facing murder charges in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings was charged on Tuesday in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama woman doing well after experimental pig kidney transplant
item.description: Towana Looney is the fifth American given a gene-edited pig organ — and notably, she isn’t as sick as prior recipients who died within two months of receiving a pig kidney or heart.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 7.3 magnitude earthquake causes widespread damage on island nation of Vanuatu
item.description: A powerful magnitude 7.3 earthquake struck just off the coast of Vanuatu on Tuesday, causing widespread destruction in the South Pacific island nation as the injured began arriving at a hospital and unconfirmed reports of casualties emerged.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Americans' trust in nation's court system hits record low, survey finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At a time of heightened political division, Americans' confidence in their country’s judicial system and courts dropped to a record low of 35% this year, according to a new Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Younger Asian Americans navigate something new to their generation: Taking up space
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The chairs stay occupied at 12 Pell. Client after client, they come through the tiny barbershop on a narrow side street in Manhattan’s Chinatown. They come for the cuts, sure. But really, they’re coming for the cool.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Teacher and student killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: A teacher and a student were killed in a shooting at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin, police said Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Devin Willems, ABC 4

item.title: Jill Jacobson, actress who appeared on 'Star Trek' and 'Falcon Crest,' dies at 70
item.description: "We are incredibly sad to say goodbye to our beautiful, soulful, hysterically funny, elegantly raunchy client, Jill Jacobson," her manager Ben Padua told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: As schools cut back on bus service, parents turn to rideshare apps
item.description: Reliance on school buses has been waning for years as districts struggle to find drivers and more students attend schools far outside their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: The original lyrics of 'Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas' were extremely depressing
item.description: “Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas” is already more melancholy than most classic Christmas songs, but its earlier version was a downright downer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: ABC settles Trump’s defamation suit for $15M
item.description: The settlement enables both the anchor and Trump to avoid sitting for court-ordered depositions next week.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: VIDEO: San Francisco cafe employee takes down would-be thief
item.description: The video (above) shows a man wearing a black hoodie and mask walking into the cafe and trying to take an item from a customer dining inside.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, ABC 4

item.title: Private Sin: How a church protected child molesters
item.description: Protestant Reformed Churches in America is struggling with what it has called the "private sin" of child sexual abuse and decades of covering up for abusers, including church leaders and school teachers.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, Fox 5

item.title: Former Manhattan prosecutor to represent suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO has added a prominent defense lawyer to his legal team as Manhattan prosecutors work to return him from Pennsylvania to face a murder charge.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a defamation lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
